This family consists of around 2,500 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Acanthaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Gymnacanthus geminiflorus being a part of the Thunbergioideae subfamily. Within the Acanthaceae family, Gymnacanthus geminiflorus is a distinct species known for its distinctive flower clusters and foliage.
